RouteChat

A spontaneous guided audio tour wherever you are, whether you are walking, driving, riding a train, biking, or moving through a place in any other way.

Problem

Most tours require planning, fixed routes, tickets, or a specific destination. People are often already moving through interesting places and would enjoy useful, surprising, context-aware narration without having to research the area first.

Idea

RouteChat is a location-aware voice companion for travelers, commuters, walkers, road trippers, cyclists, and curious locals. It generates narration from current location, movement speed, direction, and mode, then adapts as the route changes.

MVP

  • Supports walking, driving, train rides, biking, and other transit modes.
  • Generates narration from current latitude, longitude, movement direction, speed, selected mode, and recent narration history.
  • Lets users choose a tone, ask follow-up questions, pause, replay, and switch modes by voice.
  • Lets users save and replay generated tours.

Promotion Criteria

  • A clear first mode, probably walking tours or road trip narration.
  • A working prototype that can generate interesting narration from live location data.
  • A strong hands-free voice interaction design.
  • A safety model for driving and transit use.
